title: Pentanema × doellianum
intro: Pentanema × doellianum, commonly known as Doell's fleabane, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, including inflammation and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Pentanema × doellianum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Pentanema × doellianum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Pentanema × doellianum is a hybrid species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is a cross between Pentanema fruticinum and Pentanema villosum. The classification of Pentanema × doellianum is as a hybrid of the two parent species. It is often found in the Mediterranean region. The exact classification can vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted as a hybrid within the Pentanema genus.
uses: Pentanema × doellianum is a hybrid plant species, but there is limited information available on its medicinal uses. However, the closely related species Pentanema rubellum has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. The plant has been found to contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. benefits: Pentanema × doellianum has been found to have various health benefits, particularly in traditional medicine.
